extraction.one_line_summary: VALVERDE timed sprint to perfection from fifth wheel with ~200m to go after MÜHLBERGER leadout, taking 10-second bonus into race lead.
tactical_patterns: MÜHLBERGER's Bora leadout for MCCARTHY inadvertently set up VALVERDE who sat fifth-sixth wheel until final 200m headwind sprint.
Mitchelton-Scott rode tempo on second-category climb to eliminate pure sprinters for IMPEY, but headwind finish favored patient finishers.
HODEG dropped on second-category climb despite winning stage one, revealing sprint versatility doesn't predict punchy uphill tolerance.
MAISON's solo 50m gap attack with 13.7km left immediately absorbed, confirming breakaway futility before final classified climb.

rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: Demonstrated unique ability to time sprint from deep position into headwind after bonus-point collection earlier.
rider_signals.MCCARTHY Jay: positive: Second place confirms ability to handle selective climbing pace then still contest sprint with pure finishers.
rider_signals.MÜHLBERGER Gregor: neutral: Executed perfect Bora leadout for MCCARTHY but team sprint train harvested by VALVERDE's superior positioning.
rider_signals.IMPEY Daryl: neutral: Team rode aggressively to thin field but seventh shows late headwind sprint timing trumped climbing selection.
rider_signals.HODEG Álvaro José: negative: Dropped on moderate second-category climb day after winning, exposing narrow stage-winning bandwidth for pure sprinters.
rider_signals.MOHORIČ Matej: positive: Present in final sprint mix after surviving climbing pace, confirming emerging versatility beyond classics profile.
